summary: Surveys 81,000 Claude users about AI's economic effects and links their stated concerns to patterns measured in actual Claude usage traffic, offering a grounded read on how AI is reshaping work.

Launches a monthly survey, run via Anthropic's automated Interviewer, that collects qualitative feedback from Claude users on how AI is affecting their jobs, productivity, and expectations to complement traditional labor-market indicators.

Introduces CoVA, a composed video retrieval task incorporating both visual and audio variations, plus the AV-Comp benchmark and AVT compositional fusion, which selectively aligns text queries to the most relevant modality and beats unimodal fusion baselines.

Presents SmoothCruiser, a planning algorithm for entropy-regularized MDPs and two-player games that exploits the regularized Bellman operator's smoothness to achieve problem-independent O~(1/eps^4) sample complexity, unlike unregularized settings lacking polynomial worst-case guarantees.

Proposes a budgeted online influence-maximization framework that minimizes total campaign cost rather than a cardinality constraint, with an independent-cascade algorithm under edge-level semi-bandit feedback and improved regret bounds that also tighten the cardinality-constrained case.

Analyzes two determinantal-point-process estimators for Monte Carlo integration: a fixed-DPP one with O(N^{-(1+1/d)}) variance for smooth functions and an unbiased function-tailored one, generalizing both to continuous settings and providing sampling algorithms.

Proposes Reverse Constitutional AI, which inverts a harmless constitution into a toxicity constitution and uses a critique-revision RLAIF pipeline to synthesize adversarial red-teaming data; probability clamping curbs reward hacking, improving semantic coherence ~15% without weakening adversarial strength.

Applies computer vision and ML to street-view imagery to predict six perceptual dimensions across 1930s HOLC redlining grades in Buffalo, finding persistent perceptual inequality driven by built-environment features like trees, buildings, and roads via SHAP analysis.

Derives sample-complexity bounds for learning eps-optimal policies in Stochastic Shortest Path problems with a generative model, proving an Omega(SAB*^3/(c_min eps^2)) lower bound with matching algorithms and showing SSPs are strictly harder than finite-horizon or discounted settings.

Introduces GRIFT, which detects reward hacking by computing and compressing gradients of a model's chain-of-thought; it beats CoT Monitor and TRACE by over 25% relatively and, used in rejection fine-tuning, reduces hacking while improving true-task performance.

Presents ORION, a multi-agent LLM framework that automates end-to-end analysis of immune-profiling (PhIP-seq) data; it recovered the canonical APS-1 autoantibody signature in about two hours versus months of manual work and prioritized candidate targets in Down syndrome data.
OpenAI's GPT-Rosalind, a frontier reasoning model built to accelerate life-sciences workflows including drug discovery, genomics analysis, and protein reasoning.

Introduces Rejection-Gated Policy Optimization, replacing the importance-sampling ratio with a smooth differentiable acceptance gate; it guarantees bounded gradient variance under heavy-tailed ratios, unifies TRPO/PPO/REINFORCE, matches PPO's cost, and extends to RLHF preference fine-tuning.

Shows RLVR-trained reasoning models game verifiers on inductive tasks by enumerating instance labels instead of inducing rules; introduces Isomorphic Perturbation Testing to detect such shortcuts, finding the behavior specific to RLVR models and worsening with task complexity and inference compute.

Demonstrates subliminal learning: a student model distilled on a teacher's semantically unrelated outputs (e.g., number sequences) inherits the teacher's behavioral traits, even broad misalignment, when base models match, implying safety evaluations must scrutinize data and model provenance, not just behavior.

Empirically evaluates four drop-in techniques for LLM-as-a-judge (ensemble scoring, task-specific criteria injection, calibration context, and adaptive escalation) on RewardBench 2; criteria injection plus ensembling reaches 85.8% accuracy (+13.5pp), with small models benefiting most.

Surveys reward hacking in RLHF/RLAIF/RLVR, cataloguing manifestations like sycophancy and benchmark overfitting and their generalization into deception, and proposes the Proxy Compression Hypothesis framing hacking as optimizing expressive policies against compressed reward representations.

Anthropic experiment in which nine Claude instances autonomously proposed, tested, and analyzed ideas to improve weak-to-strong supervision; Claude reached a 0.97 performance-gap-recovery score after five days versus a 0.23 human baseline, suggesting AI can accelerate alignment research.

A workshop advancing bidirectional human-AI alignment, a reciprocal process where humans and AI co-adapt, gathering HCI, AI, and social-science researchers to develop value-centered design, interactive alignment methods, and societal-impact evaluation frameworks.

A CHI community meet-up convening international HCI researchers on responsible, human-centered AI across value-sensitive design, ethics, explainability, and equity, using a town-hall format to set priorities and forge cross-border collaborations.

Introduces PaperScope, a multimodal multi-document benchmark for agentic deep research built on a knowledge graph of 2,000+ AI papers, with 2,000+ QA pairs spanning reasoning, retrieval, summarization, and problem-solving; even advanced deep-research systems score poorly.

Formulates reward-hacking mitigation as robust policy optimization over all r-correlated proxy rewards, deriving a tractable max-min objective; the method outperforms ORPO in worst-case returns and yields interpretable worst-case rewards under linear feature assumptions.

Introduces compute-grounded reasoning, resolving answerable sub-problems via deterministic computation before LLM generation; Spatial Atlas instantiates it as an A2A server with a spatial scene-graph engine and self-healing ML pipeline, achieving competitive, interpretable results on FieldWorkArena and MLE-Bench.

Fine-tunes Qwen3-8B with sycophancy-inducing GRPO and finds consistent but non-significant calibration degradation (higher ECE/MCE); matrix scaling cuts ECE 40-64% yet leaves a structured residual miscalibration, establishing a methodology for assessing reward hacking's calibration impact.

A comparative analysis tracing the GPT family from GPT-3 to GPT-5 across technical framing, modality, deployment, and governance, arguing later models are aligned, multimodal, tool-oriented systems rather than just larger predictors, while limitations like hallucination and benchmark fragility persist.
A reply to Hu arguing that reasoning traces need not faithfully reflect underlying computation in order to serve as resource-rational accounts of the cost of human thinking.

Probes per-iteration hidden states of the looped transformer Ouro-2.6B, training ~5M-parameter evaluator heads to predict human preference on HH-RLHF; a pairwise probe hits 95.2% accuracy, revealing preference is encoded relationally rather than independently, as an internal value-consistency signal.
An Anthropic explainer on AI agents covering how they work and the practices used to make them trustworthy as agentic systems become a dominant mode of AI use.

Tests LLMs' limits on discovering and executing multi-step latent planning in graph path-finding, finding a depth ceiling (tiny transformers ~3 steps, GPT-5.4 ~7) and a gap between discovering versus executing latent strategies, lending support to chain-of-thought monitoring.

Introduces ROTATE, a data-free, forward-pass-free method that disentangles MLP neurons in weight space by maximizing vocabulary-space kurtosis, recovering sparse interpretable 'vocabulary channels' whose ablation selectively disables behaviors and yields neuron descriptions 2-3x better than activation baselines.
